subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **40 character cards** per player (identical sets across all players, numbered 1-40)
- **Loot tokens** (various types with different end-of-voyage effects)
- **Reputation track board**
- **Score track** (doubloons)
- **Player boards** (ship tableau with day/dusk/night sections)
- **Midshipman token** (first player marker)
- **Reputation tokens** (1 per player)
- **Graveyard area** on each player board
- **Loot bag**

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Place the **central island board** in the middle of the table.
2. Set all players' **reputation markers** to the center of the reputation track.
3. Place the **score track** (doubloons) where all players can see it; all players start at 0.
4. Shuffle **loot tokens** face-down. For each day in the voyage, draw loot tokens equal to the player count and place them face-up on the island.
5. Prepare the loot tokens for all 3 voyages (6 days per voyage = 6 rows of loot).

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Each player takes an **identical set of character cards** (all 40 characters).
2. Randomly deal **6 character cards** to one player. That player keeps these 6 cards, and **all other players find the same 6 characters** in their own decks. Everyone starts with the same hand.
3. Each player takes a **player board** to organize their tableau.
4. Set each player's **doubloons** to the starting amount (typically 0).
5. Choose a starting player; give them the **Midshipman token**.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
Libertalia: Winds of Galecrest is played over **3 voyages**, each consisting of **4-6 days**. Every day, players simultaneously select a character card from their hand and reveal. Characters resolve in rank order, using unique abilities to collect loot, gain doubloons, manipulate opponents, or cause chaos.

The core tension: all players have the **same characters** available, so you must outguess your opponents on which character to play and when.

After 3 voyages, the player with the **most doubloons** wins.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each **day** within a voyage follows this structure:

### 1. Sunrise — Select Characters
All players simultaneously choose **one character card** from their hand and place it face-down. Once all players are ready, reveal simultaneously.

### 2. Daytime — Resolve Day Abilities (Low to High)
Characters with **daytime abilities** resolve from **lowest rank to highest**. These abilities often involve attacking, manipulating the board, or gaining advantages.

### 3. Dusk — Collect Loot (High to Low)
Starting with the **highest-ranked** character and going down, each player picks **one loot token** from the island display. Higher-rank characters get first pick of the best loot.

### 4. Night — Resolve Night Abilities
After loot is collected, characters with **night abilities** resolve. These often trigger based on what loot you took or your board state.

### 5. Rest
Played character cards go to your **ship** (tableau area). Some characters have ongoing effects or end-of-voyage scoring. Proceed to the next day.

### End of Voyage
After all days in a voyage are complete:
- Resolve all **anchor abilities** (end-of-voyage effects on cards in your ship).
- Resolve all **loot token effects** — many loot tokens grant or deduct doubloons at voyage end.
- Discard loot tokens. Characters in your ship go to your **graveyard** (discard).
- Deal **new character cards** for the next voyage. Players keep any leftover cards from previous voyages, so hands grow over the game.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
### Character Abilities
Each of the 40 characters has a unique combination of abilities that can trigger at different times:
- **Daytime** (sun icon): Resolves low-to-high. Often aggressive or manipulative.
- **Dusk** (sunset icon): Triggers when collecting loot.
- **Night** (moon icon): Triggers after loot collection.
- **Anchor** (anchor icon): Triggers at end of voyage if the card is in your ship.
- **Graveyard**: Some characters have effects when discarded.

### Loot Tokens
Loot tokens have various effects:
- **Treasure chests**: Worth doubloons at end of voyage.
- **Maps**: Worth doubloons based on how many you collect (set collection).
- **Cursed relics**: Negative doubloons — but some characters want them.
- **Sabers**: Allow combat bonuses.
- **Special loot**: Unique effects like stealing or swapping.

### Reputation Track
Some abilities reference your **reputation**. Moving up the reputation track breaks ties (higher reputation acts first among same-rank characters). Some loot and abilities move you on this track.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
### Tie-Breaking
If two players play the same character (same rank), the player with **higher reputation** resolves first.

### Growing Hands
In Voyage 1, you start with 6 cards. Before Voyage 2, you receive 6 new cards but keep leftover cards from Voyage 1. Before Voyage 3, you receive 6 more new cards. Your hand grows, giving you more options but also more information about what opponents might play.

### Card Memory
Since all players receive the same cards, you can track what opponents have played and deduce what remains in their hands.

### Graveyard Effects
Some characters trigger abilities from the graveyard. Characters in the graveyard are visible to all players.

### Solo Mode
The game includes an automa system for solo play against a simulated opponent.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
After the **3rd voyage** ends and all anchor and loot effects are resolved:

- Players total their **doubloons** from the score track.
- The player with the **most doubloons** wins.
- Ties are broken by **reputation** (higher reputation wins).

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **High-rank characters get first pick of loot** but resolve daytime abilities last. Low-rank characters act first in daytime but get worse loot.
- **Read your entire hand** before each day. Know what all your characters do so you can plan ahead.
- **Don't always play your strongest card**. If everyone plays high, your medium card might get surprisingly good loot.
- **Pay attention to loot tokens** on the island. Choose your character based on what loot is available that day.
- **Track what others have played** — if you know someone no longer has a key character, you can play accordingly.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Loot reading**: Always check the available loot before choosing your character. If the loot is mediocre, play a low-rank character with a strong daytime ability instead.
- **Mirroring and counter-play**: Since everyone has the same cards, think about what your opponents are likely to play and counter it.
- **Save strong cards for high-value loot days**: Don't waste your best characters on weak loot. Save high-rank cards for days with treasure chests or maps.
- **Reputation management**: Moving up the reputation track gives you consistent tie-breaking advantage. Prioritize this in close games.
- **Set collection**: Maps and matching loot sets can be worth enormous points. Commit to a loot strategy and draft accordingly.
- **Graveyard planning**: Some late-game characters combo with what's in your graveyard. Plan your play order across the whole voyage.
